prep.summary: Review the June 3rd alignment meeting between Mark and Ramesh—the critical precursor to the Rapid Architecture Governance readout. The Most Important Thing Today—the MITT—is securing Mark's attendance at the joint tech and business readout to navigate the intense organizational tension over the Agent Assist UI. Key takeaways focus on the unresolved dispute between the CTO's office and business stakeholders over Out-of-the-Box versus embedded UI options—an architectural conflict requiring immediate alignment.
tasks: Attend the joint technology and business readout session to address organizational storming around the Agent Assist architecture.
Resolve the UI option conflict—specifically evaluating the Amazon Out-of-the-Box versus the embedded widget solutions—before tech leadership prematurely eliminates viable paths.
Clarify the 'Path to Production' slide messaging from the James D&S session to prevent further misinterpretation by leadership regarding the decommissioning of Conversant.
Establish a consistent engagement process with business partners to enforce accountability for process requirements and ownership.
prep.title: Sync with Mark | Amit Baviskar (Follow-up)

events.summary: EMG Engage - Intro to Architecture w/Mark Francis |
REF: Debrief on ServiceNow: P&C & LifeCo Alignments

prep.summary: You must track several critical architectural shifts across your portfolio—including the onboarding of Suresh's Transformation Office using Scan AI, the upcoming Project IVA member rollout, and the rising integration challenges from Salesforce's Informatica acquisition. Address the escalating data architecture skill gaps in P&C—where integration architects require tight pairing with staff engineers to prevent project failure. Drive alignment on the ServiceNow event-monitoring proof of concept and establish clear governance rules for developer tool usage.
tasks: Escalate the operational risks associated with the P&C data architecture skill gaps—ensuring integration architects are systematically paired with staff engineers to mitigate design failures.
Revise the ServiceNow event-monitoring presentation deck for Dan Griffiths—specifically hiding slides 6, 7, 15, and 515 to streamline the narrative.
Enforce strict accountability rules for developers utilizing personal AI productivity tools—mandating complete manual validation of all generated code before production deployment.
Confirm the P&C funding commitments for the subsequent phases of the Apache Flink real-time pipeline deployment—adhering to the established line-of-business onboarding schedules.
Monitor the progress of the express RFP for Scan AI licensing—ensuring Sean Garner captures cross-functional requirements across both Bank and P&C.
prep.title: Weekly Staff – P&C CTO Leadership

focus_html:
Focus · Next up
8:30 AM — 9:00 AM

Sync with Mark | Amit Baviskar (Follow-up)

Mark's office:; EXEC SAT HO (B)
Prep

Review the June 3rd alignment meeting between Mark and Ramesh—the critical precursor to the Rapid Architecture Governance readout. The Most Important Thing Today—the MITT—is securing Mark's attendance at the joint tech and business readout to navigate the intense organizational tension over the Agent Assist UI. Key takeaways focus on the unresolved dispute between the CTO's office and business stakeholders over Out-of-the-Box versus embedded UI options—an architectural conflict requiring immediate alignment.

  • Attend the joint technology and business readout session to address organizational storming around the Agent Assist architecture.
  • Resolve the UI option conflict—specifically evaluating the Amazon Out-of-the-Box versus the embedded widget solutions—before tech leadership prematurely eliminates viable paths.
  • Clarify the 'Path to Production' slide messaging from the James D&S session to prevent further misinterpretation by leadership regarding the decommissioning of Conversant.
  • Establish a consistent engagement process with business partners to enforce accountability for process requirements and ownership.
